steps.title: Locate the main verb
steps.content: The main verb of the sentence is "cannot stand." The words before it need to form a subject phrase about a hypothesis suggesting that certain trees survived. The blank should modify "hypothesis," not create a separate main verb.

steps.title: Use the participle
steps.content: Choice B, Choice C, and Choice D are finite verb forms that would create a competing clause before "cannot stand." The remaining present participle makes "suggesting that" modify "hypothesis," forming a complete subject phrase. The answer is A.
